column1: #mapping_set_description: Manually curated SSSOM mappings between FINOS CDM classes and the Gist upper business ontology (lmodel/gist).

column9: comment
CDM Party subsumes legal entities and natural persons; gist Organization is the closest formal-business concept
CDM Party also covers natural persons
CDM LegalEntity is a formally-recognised organisation
gist GovernmentOrganization is a narrower kind of LegalEntity
Both represent a human individual
Both model a financial or custodial account
Both abstract over physical and electronic addresses
CDM Address can carry physical-address fields
CDM ContactInformation holds email/phone/electronic endpoints
A business unit is an organisational subdivision
gist ID represents an externally-issued identifier
Identifier issued for a party
Identifier issued for a natural person
Identifier issued for a legal entity (e.g. LEI)
Versioned identifier assignment
gist Magnitude (value + unit) covers monetary amounts when unit is a currency
A cash position is a currency-denominated magnitude
CDM Price is a typed magnitude in market terms
Magnitude with unit-of-measure
A measured value with unit
CDM UnitType selects a unit of measure
A duration / time interval
CDM Schedule lists step events; gist ScheduledEvent models a single planned occurrence
A trade is a financial transaction
TradeState wraps a Trade with state metadata
Both represent a bilateral/multilateral agreement
CDM MasterAgreement is a specific subtype (e.g. ISDA Master)
A lifecycle event in the business sense
Workflow groups tasks; gist Task models a unit of work
gist Assignment binds a Party to a role/responsibility
Counterparty assigns a Party to a buyer/seller role
Role assignment for a natural person
Specification of acceptable collateral
Collateral provisions are contract terms
Specification of a tradable product
